Package simulation

Class EventScheduler

java.lang.Object
simulation.EventScheduler
public final class EventScheduler extends Object
Priority-queue–based event scheduler for the discrete-event simulation.

Replaces the hand-rolled sorted linked list in the legacy Events class. Events are dequeued in ascending time order; ties are broken by insertion order (natural FIFO for equal timestamps, since PriorityQueue is a min-heap).

Event fields

Each EventScheduler.Event carries:
  • time — the simulation tick at which this event fires.
  • nodeId — the entity to act on (sensor-node ID or 747 for the UAV/drone).
  • motionState — the Markov-chain state at the time of the event (0 = IDLE, 1 = MOVE, 2 = CHANGE_DIR, 3 = CONTINUE).

    Returns true if no events are currently scheduled.
    EventScheduler.Event

    Returns the earliest-scheduled event without removing it, or null if the queue is empty.

    Removes and returns the earliest-scheduled event, or null if the queue is empty.

    • EventScheduler

      public EventScheduler()
      Constructs an empty scheduler.

  • Method Details

    • schedule

      public void schedule(long time, int nodeId, int motionState)
      Schedules a new event.
      Parameters:
      time - simulation tick at which the event should fire
      nodeId - entity ID targeted by the event
      motionState - Markov-chain motion state (0–3)

    • peek

      public EventScheduler.Event peek()
      Returns the earliest-scheduled event without removing it, or null if the queue is empty.
      Returns:
      next event, or null

    • poll

      public EventScheduler.Event poll()
      Removes and returns the earliest-scheduled event, or null if the queue is empty.
      Returns:
      next event, or null

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if no events are currently scheduled.
      Returns:
      true when the queue is empty

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of pending events.
      Returns:
      pending event count
